hero.eyebrow: MRR dashboard for solo SaaS founders
hero.headline: See your real MRR without logging into Stripe
hero.subheadline: Connect a read-only Stripe key and get MRR, churn, failed payments, and forecasts on one page. 30 seconds to set up, no webhooks required.
hero.image_prompt: Editorial magazine-cover illustration, dark mode. A minimalist isometric composition of thin concentric rings representing revenue cohorts, stacked like a topographic map on a deep charcoal background (#0B0D10). A single thin vertical beam of warm amber (#F4A261) cuts upward through the rings, suggesting MRR growth. Around it, scattered small geometric shards in muted teal (#2A9D8F) imply churn and failed charges drifting away. Subtle film grain, crisp vector geometry, long soft shadows, sense of depth. No text, no UI elements, no people, no logos, no swooshes.

problem.title: Stripe buries the numbers you actually check every morning.
scenarios: You open Stripe to check yesterday's MRR. Four clicks later you're staring at a chart that bundles trials with paid subs and annual plans are counted at full value instead of monthly.
A customer churned last night. To find out who, why, and how long they'd been paying, you cross-reference three screens and export a CSV. It's 8am and you haven't had coffee.
Two payments failed this week. Stripe didn't tell you. You'll notice when the monthly email summary lands — or when the customer emails to cancel.

solution.title: One page. Your real numbers. No Stripe tabs open.
solution.body: Paste a read-only restricted API key. Within 30 seconds you see true MRR (annual plans divided by 12, trials excluded), 7/30/90-day deltas, yesterday's churn with customer name and tenure, every subscription currently in payment failure, and a forecast for next month based on your active book. We poll Stripe every 15 minutes and cache locally, so no webhooks to configure and no Stripe dashboard tabs to keep open. Read-only means we literally cannot charge, refund, or modify anything in your account. If your key leaked tomorrow, the worst case is someone else sees your numbers.

features.title: True MRR, computed correctly
features.body: Annual plans divided by 12. Trials excluded. Discounts applied. Quarterly and custom intervals normalized to monthly. The number you'd compute yourself in a spreadsheet, computed every 15 minutes so you don't have to.

features.title: Churn with context
features.body: Yesterday's cancellations listed by customer name, plan, MRR lost, and tenure. If Stripe captured a cancellation reason, it's there. No more exporting CSVs to figure out who you lost and whether they were a six-month or six-year customer.

features.title: Payment failure alerts
features.body: Every subscription currently in past_due or unpaid status, surfaced on the dashboard. Optional email when a new failure appears so you can reach out before the retry exhausts and the customer involuntarily churns.

features.title: Read-only by design
features.body: We ask for a restricted Stripe key with read scopes only. We cannot charge customers, issue refunds, or modify subscriptions. The attack surface is your numbers, not your money. Revoke the key from Stripe anytime and we go dark immediately.

features.title: No webhook setup
features.body: Most dashboards ask you to configure webhooks, verify signing secrets, and debug missed events. We poll the Stripe API every 15 minutes and reconcile state each time. Connect a key, close the tab, come back to a working dashboard.

features.title: Next-month forecast
features.body: Based on active subscriptions, billing cycles, known cancellations, and historical involuntary churn rate on your account. Not a black-box ML model — a transparent calculation you can audit. See the assumptions, adjust if you disagree.

who_this_is_for.title: You're in the right place if…
items: You're a solo or two-person SaaS founder doing between $1k and $50k MRR on Stripe.
You already check Stripe daily and want the numbers visible without five clicks and a mental calculator.
You've tried Baremetrics or ChartMogul and bounced off the $100+/mo pricing for a one-person business.
You don't need team seats, Slack integrations, cohort retention heatmaps, or a customer success rep onboarding you.

pricing.tagline: Honest pricing. Cancel anytime from the dashboard.

tiers.name: Starter
tiers.description: For one Stripe account and recent history.

tiers.name: Pro
tiers.description: For founders running multiple products or brands.

faq.q: How is this different from Baremetrics or ChartMogul?
faq.a: They're built for growth-stage teams with revenue ops people. You get cohort heatmaps, segmentation, Slack digests, and a $100-250/mo bill. This is the 20% of that product a solo founder actually opens daily: MRR, churn, failures, forecast. One page, $9 or $29/mo. If you outgrow it and need cohort LTV analysis across ten dimensions, go use Baremetrics — no hard feelings.

faq.q: Is my Stripe data actually safe?
faq.a: We require a restricted Stripe key with read-only scopes. We cannot create charges, issue refunds, modify subscriptions, or update customers — Stripe enforces this at the API level, not us. Keys are encrypted at rest. Revoke the key in your Stripe dashboard and our access ends that second. We store aggregated metrics and cached subscription state, nothing is sold or shared with third parties.

Webhooks require you to configure an endpoint, copy a signing secret, and debug missed events when Stripe retries fail. For a solo founder that's 20 minutes of friction before you see anything. We poll the Stripe API every 15 minutes and reconcile full state, so setup is pasting one API key. The tradeoff is up to 15 minutes of lag on brand-new events, which doesn't matter for a daily-check dashboard.

faq.q: Do you support Paddle, Chargebee, or Lemon Squeezy?
faq.a: No, Stripe only. The product is called Stripe Revenue Tracker. Supporting other processors means reconciling different data models, which would compromise how accurate the Stripe experience is. If you're on Paddle or Chargebee, this isn't the right tool — and we'd rather tell you upfront than sell you a subscription you'll cancel.

faq.q: What counts as MRR here? Are annual plans included?
faq.a: Yes. Annual plans are divided by 12 and counted as monthly recurring revenue. Quarterly and semi-annual plans are normalized the same way. Trials are excluded until they convert. Discounts and coupons are applied to the recognized amount. One-time payments don't count. We show the formula on the dashboard so you can audit any number against Stripe yourself.

faq.q: How long does setup actually take?
faq.a: Under a minute if you already have a Stripe account. Sign up, go to Stripe's API keys page, create a restricted key with read-only permissions (we give you a one-click preset link), paste it, done. First sync pulls your last 90 days of subscription history in about 20-60 seconds depending on volume, and the dashboard loads with real numbers immediately after.

Yes, 14 days free on either tier. No credit card required to start — connect your Stripe key, see your numbers, decide if it's worth $9 or $29/mo. If you subscribe and change your mind in the first 30 days, email us for a full refund.

Yes. Pro includes CSV export of subscriptions, MRR history, churn events, and failed payments. Starter gets CSV export of the current dashboard view. Everything we show you is derived from Stripe, so you can always go back to Stripe as the source of truth — we're a view, not a system of record.

cta_final.headline: Stop clicking through five Stripe screens to check MRR.
cta_final.body: Connect a read-only key, see your numbers in 30 seconds, cancel anytime if it's not useful.
cta_final.urgency: 14-day free trial, no credit card required.
